factors.name: AI Semiconductor Demand Surge
factors.summary: Samsung expected to post 18-fold profit jump on AI memory demand (per Bloomberg). SK Hynix planning $29B US listing to compete in AI chip market; TSMC Citi upgrade signals 2026 guidance raise on AI demand. Memory pricing strength validates earnings momentum vs. speculation.

factors.name: Hyperscaler Capex Momentum
factors.summary: Anthropic committing $15B USD for 1.4-GW Australian data centre capacity (AFR), signaling sustained cloud/AI infrastructure investment. Foxconn Q2 revenue surges on AI boost despite geopolitical warnings. Forward guidance on capex will be critical watch ahead of earnings.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Easing
factors.summary: Gold prices rising and rate-hike bets cooling (per Bloomberg Markets). Reduced expectations for Fed hikes support long-duration growth multiples critical to NAS100. Real yields have likely compressed, benefiting tech valuations.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran-US Deal Holds)
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening continues; tankers transiting via US-protected corridor. Oil prices collapsing on overstocking (per TotalEnergies CEO), reducing stagflation risk and supporting risk-on sentiment. De-escalation trades favor equities.

factors.name: China Macro Softness & Yuan Weakness
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8066 vs prior 6.7780, signaling yuan weakness. China's slower domestic growth and Beijing signalling openness to foreign capital suggest growth concerns. Heavy NAS100 exposure to China-dependent tech could drag.

factors.name: Asian Chip Stock Pullback After Rebound
factors.summary: Japan, South Korea chipmakers pulling back after recent rebound (per MarketWatch). Samsung/SK Hynix leveraged ETF volatility warnings from Bank of Korea signal potential regulatory tightening and profit-taking risk.

factors.name: Short-Term Momentum & Valuation Stretch
factors.summary: Strategist warns momentum trades face violent unwind risk in July (MarketWatch). NAS100 +0.44% in 4h after -1.24% 24h suggests choppy price action. Mega-cap tech multiples already priced for AI growth; limited margin of safety.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Real Yields Neutral-to-Firm
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 161.70, JGB 10-yr yields 2.79% (30-yr high). Euro consolidating below 1.1400. If real yields stabilize or tick higher, long-duration growth stocks face headwind; if yields continue lower, equities rallied harder.

factors.name: Limited Near-Term Data Catalyst
factors.summary: No major CPI, Fed minutes, or employment data in immediate 24-72h window. Bond auctions (10/30Y Treasuries) this week are moderate-risk. Earnings season still ramping; NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL/AAPL dates critical but not imminent.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: (1) genuine AI semiconductor earnings momentum (Samsung 18x profit jump, TSMC upgrade, hyperscaler capex acceleration) and (2) extended momentum rally facing profit-taking and geopolitical/macro headwinds. The recent 4h +0.44% bounce off a 24h -1.24% decline suggests choppy consolidation rather than directional conviction. De-escalation of Iran tensions and cooling rate-hike expectations provide tailwinds, but China's Yuan weakness, Asian chip stock pullbacks, and July seasonality warnings flag caution. Mega-cap tech valuations remain stretched, and sentiment-driven reversals in momentum trades could unwind quickly ahead of earnings season.
catalysts_ahead: Fed June FOMC minutes (early week)
10-year & 30-year Treasury auctions (week of July 6)
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, AMZN earnings (mid-to-late July)
Samsung earnings confirmation of memory profit beat (July 8-9)
US jobs report (early July if scheduled)
Any escalation in Strait of Hormuz tensions or geopolitical flashpoint
ECB communications on rate path (Nagel, Moulin commentary watch)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed minutes signal renewed hawkishness or inflation resilience, real yields could spike, crushing long-duration growth multiples and triggering NAS100 selloff. Conversely, if mega-cap earnings disappoint on AI capex outlook or demand slowdown signals, even dovish Fed support may not sustain rally. China stimulus reversal, Taiwan tensions escalation, or renewed Hormuz disruption are geopolitical wildcards. Momentum unwind into July could be violent—watch single-stock gamma in NVDA/MSFT/AAPL for dealer position shifts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 with mixed signals. Wait for pullback to 29100 support or confirmation break above 29600 resistance before committing size. Given earnings-driven volatility ahead and extended 4h positioning, scale in rather than chase. Watch Fed minutes release for directional clarity.

factors.name: Nvidia AI chip delay (SemiAnalysis)
factors.summary: Next-gen AI server rack delayed to 2028 due to manufacturing snags, raising concerns about Nvidia's annual release cadence hitting manufacturing limits. Critical headwind for NDX's heaviest AI darling.

factors.name: Dovish repricing post-NFP (soft payrolls)
factors.summary: Last week's mixed June NFP triggered dovish repricing; July Fed hike odds fell to 24%, September to 55%. Lower rate expectations typically support long-duration growth multiples in Nasdaq.

factors.name: Tech earnings season imminent (Samsung, NVDA next)
factors.summary: Samsung results Tuesday are described as 'high-stakes' validation for AI trade; NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings coming this week/next. Outcome could swing NDX 2–3%+ either direction.

factors.name: Real yields (DXY, USD strength)
factors.summary: DXY holding near 101.00; USD/JPY rallying to 162.20 on rate-hike expectations despite soft jobs data. Stronger dollar and sticky real yields create headwind for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Oil glut fears, OPEC+ raise output
factors.summary: Oil down >1% post-OPEC+ agreement to raise output; oil glut concerns rising. Typically supports growth multiples via lower energy costs, but geopolitical risk (Hormuz, China missile test) adds noise.

factors.name: FOMC minutes due this week
factors.summary: Markets await FOMC minutes as major catalyst for Fed narrative and rate expectations. Could repricing real yields sharply—timing and surprise direction unknown.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech positioning & gamma risk
factors.summary: No specific data on dealer gamma or options flows in news; however, earnings season concentration risk is extremely high—single stock beats/misses can whipsaw NQ 1–2%.

factors.name: CPI report due Tuesday (next day)
factors.summary: US CPI data is high-impact catalyst scheduled for next Tuesday; currently pricing in core PCE expectations. Inflation surprise either direction would override current dovish repricing.

factors.name: 4h/24h price action—modest moves
factors.summary: 4h change +0.44%, 24h change -1.24%—small consolidation range, no strong directional commitment. Markets in wait-and-see mode into earnings and FOMC.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq 100 is consolidating after last week's strong rally (+largest weekly gain since early May per Deutsche Bank) driven by dovish repricing post-soft NFP. However, the rally is now colliding with two major headwinds: (1) Nvidia's reported AI server delay to 2028 undermines confidence in the near-term AI capex super-cycle, and (2) real yields remain sticky as the USD holds firm near 101.00. This week's earnings (Samsung Tue, NVDA/MSFT/AAPL soon after) and FOMC minutes are binary catalysts that could reignite momentum or trigger a pullback. Current price action is directionless—neither extension of the rally nor a reversal has gained traction.
catalysts_ahead: Samsung earnings (Tuesday 2026-07-07)
FOMC minutes (expected this week)
US CPI (Tuesday 2026-07-08)
US ISM services PMI (Monday 2026-07-06 today)
ADP employment data (Tuesday 2026-07-07)
RBNZ policy decision (Wednesday 2026-07-08)
Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Google earnings (likely mid-late week)
screening.risk_warning: If CPI comes in hot (>3.5% headline, >3.2% core), dovish repricing unwinds and real yields spike—expect sharp NDX selloff and move toward support. If Samsung/NVDA earnings disappoint on demand or guidance, AI narrative cracks and leadership rotates out of mega-cap tech. Conversely, if FOMC minutes appear more dovish or earnings beat with strong guidance, resistance at 29,600+ breaks and rally resumes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is incremental and directionless; avoid chasing. Wait for either confirmation above 29,600 on earnings beat + dovish catalyst, or retest of support near 29,150 for counter-trend long. CPI and FOMC minutes are imminent—cap position size into these events.

factors.name: Tech futures strength into open
factors.summary: Nasdaq 100 futures advanced 1.03% to ~29,860 during European trading (per FXStreet 08:34 UTC), signaling a tech-led rebound bid to start the week after Friday's -1.24% slide.

factors.name: AI capex tailwinds / hyperscaler rotation narrative
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley (12:09 UTC) flags AI hyperscalers poised for 'big comeback rally' as semiconductor trade cools. SK Hynix $28B US listing this week seen as AI memory demand signal. TeraWulf +20% on Anthropic data center lease highlights capex acceleration.

factors.name: Oil price collapse / macro tailwind
factors.summary: WTI down to $68.30 (-0.64%), Saudi Arabia made largest crude cut in 26 years; Evercore ISI notes oil exit from 'danger zone' is tailwind for economy and equity multiples. Reduces stagflation fears.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: DXY retook 101.00 (FXStreet 09:06 UTC), USD/JPY at ~162.30 near 40-year highs. Stronger dollar typically headwind for NQ international revenue exposure and reduces relative attractiveness of US growth multiples abroad.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity unclear
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y Treasury or TIPS move reported in news window. Last week's NFP was dovish (July hike odds at 24%, September at 55%), which should support growth multiples, but data dependency into CPI week (likely Wed/Thu) keeps positioning fluid.

factors.name: Crypto / risk-on sentiment mixed
factors.summary: Bitcoin bounced to best week since March, but Michael Saylor's Strategy sold record 3,588 BTC ($216M) to fund dividends—potential liquidation headwind. Crypto strength historically supports NQ, but forced selling is a caution.

factors.name: Quant rotation / momentum fade
factors.summary: Bloomberg (11:55 UTC) reports quant hedge funds extending worst run since 2023 as momentum slides; violent rotation in underbelly of bull market suggests positioning fragility and potential for sharp reversals.

factors.name: Semis / NVDA outlook uncertain
factors.summary: ASML target raised at Bernstein on capex cycle intensity, but no fresh NVDA guidance or earnings news. Samsung reportedly proposing 20% ASP hike signals tight supply but also margin pressure risk.

factors.name: CPI data imminent (likely mid-week)
factors.summary: No CPI print in news window yet; critical inflation data due soon. Current market repricing dovish after NFP, but a CPI beat could quickly flip rate expectations and compress multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical noise (NATO summit, Iran/Hormuz, Ukraine)
factors.summary: Trump heading to NATO summit, Ukraine strike/Russia barrage, Iran control of Strait of Hormuz remain live. Tanker traffic restored, but geopolitical premium remains; unlikely major NQ driver unless escalation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is rebounding early Monday on the back of a dovish NFP repricing and rotation narrative from growth-heavy tech to AI hyperscalers. Oil's sharp pullback removes stagflation tail-risk, and SK Hynix IPO / Anthropic capex signals reinvigorate the AI infrastructure story. However, the move is already ~1% into futures and price is only +0.44% in 4h—phase 2 territory. Real-yield sensitivity remains acute into this week's CPI data, and quant-fund momentum fade plus MSTR's forced BTC liquidations suggest positioning fragility.
catalysts_ahead: CPI / Core CPI release (likely Wed or Thu this week) — critical for Fed repricing and multiple re-rating
SK Hynix US ADR listing (expected this week) — $28B IPO, major AI capex signal
ISM Services PMI (likely later this week or next) — gauges services inflation and margin outlook
Treasury curve repricing into CPI — watch 10Y yield moves for real-yield impact on NQ multiples
FOMC meeting (late July or August) — forward guidance on terminal rate remain uncertain given NFP miss
screening.risk_warning: Move is already underway (+1% in futures); CPI miss (hotter inflation) could trigger sharp multiple compression and reversal. Quant momentum fade + MSTR liquidations suggest leverage is weak; any sharp reversal in risk sentiment (geopolitical shock, banking stress) could cascade. USD strength and real-yield re-pricing (if CPI surprises hot) would be immediate headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway in phase 2; avoid chasing breakouts. Wait for retest of support ~29,100–29,200 or for CPI print clarity before establishing fresh longs. If CPI data delays, watch for consolidation near 29,300–29,400 before committing.

factors.name: Trump Momentum & Policy Support
factors.summary: President Trump rang opening bell from Oval Office launching 'Trump Accounts' investment program and publicly stated 'stock market is going to go through the roof.' This direct executive endorsement and retail-oriented policy (accounts for children under 18) provides near-term sentiment lift.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Weakness Offsetting Rally
factors.summary: Microsoft announced 4,800 job cuts and Xbox unit overhaul with gaming studio spinoffs (16:14 news), directly impacting one of NDX's three largest components. This is a notable negative for breadth despite broader index opening strength.

factors.name: Semiconductor Tailwinds (AI Infrastructure)
factors.summary: TeraWulf surged 80% YTD after securing $19B Anthropic data center lease; Broadcom secured Apple supplier agreement through 2031; Micron gaining as 'return to optimism' for chip sector reported. Heavy NDX semiconductor exposure (NVDA, QCOM, AVGO, INTC) benefits from AI capex acceleration.

factors.name: Services PMI & Economic Activity Softening
factors.summary: ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI came in at 54.0 (flat vs. 54.0 estimate, down from 54.5 prior), business activity fell to 55.4 from 57.7, and Canadian services PMI collapsed to 47.1 contraction (50.6 prior). Indicates cyclical weakness broadening beyond manufacturing.

factors.name: Fed Policy Ambiguity Remains
factors.summary: Fed's Christopher Waller spoke on monetary policy transmission, reaffirming 2% inflation credibility and noting risks have 'flipped around' with labor stabilizing and inflation 'taking off.' Forward guidance debate continues; no imminent rate move clarity but language leans cautious on hold.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Sensitivity Stable
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield and real yields (TIPS) not decisively moving in today's news window. Euro weakness vs. USD (EUR/USD ~1.1421, down 0.12%) is mild; no sharp yield shock evident. Long-duration growth multiples (NDX core) not under acute compression pressure.

factors.name: Crypto-Friendly Sentiment (Trump Endorsement)
factors.summary: Trump confirmed 'big fan of crypto,' stated Bitcoin could be included in Trump Accounts, and SpaceX's Shotwell pledged stock donations. Strong crypto-positive messaging reduces regulatory tail risk and may attract retail flows into tech/growth.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk (Iran Ceasefire Unwind)
factors.summary: Bank of Canada noted Iran ceasefire (mid-June) spiked inflation expectations initially; now oil is retreating and stagflation fears easing. ECB's Schnabel notes 'current shock cannot simply be looked through,' but second-round effects remain contained so far.

factors.name: Premarket-to-Open Disappointment (Phase 2 Signal)
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures were up 325+ points premarket but actual open showed only +140 points gain (only 43% of premarket move realized). Classic sell-the-news pattern; suggests enthusiasm is waning into higher prices and some profit-taking already underway.

factors.name: Broadcom-Apple Long-Term Supply Lock
factors.summary: Broadcom (AVGO, ~4% of NDX) secured multi-year Apple supplier agreement through 2031, de-risking revenue stream and signaling strong iPhone/AI device cycle confidence through end of decade.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq opened with Trump-driven retail-friendly policy support (Trump Accounts, crypto endorsement) lifting sentiment, but gains have already faded from premarket levels—a classic 'sell the news' setup in PHASE_2. Mega-cap tech earnings risk (Microsoft job cuts announced) is weighing on breadth, while semiconductor strength (TeraWulf, Broadcom, Micron) provides some cushion. Services PMI softer than expected and Canadian economic contraction signal cyclical headwinds, pushing back against pure 'risk-on' rallies. Fed remains on hold with no clear policy shift, keeping duration sensitivity neutral.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes / Powell speak (week of July 7–11 likely)
Weekly jobless claims (Thursdays)
CPI / Core CPI release (mid-month, July 11 typical)
NVDA / MSFT / AAPL / GOOGL earnings (Q2 earnings season ramping late July)
Next FOMC decision (late July, 25th typical)
TSMC earnings/commentary (guidance on AI chip demand)
Potential trade/China tariff announcements (Trump policy risk)
screening.risk_warning: Microsoft-size tech selloff could cascade across NDX; PMI downturn broadening signals recession fears may reignite if next jobs report disappoints; Trump policy euphoria could fade on antitrust concerns (DOJ/FTC scrutiny of mega-caps); real yields spike if inflation data surprises hot could compress high-multiple growth stocks; Phase_2 momentum fade suggests rally may stall into resistance, invalidating near-term BUY bias.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (+0.53% on open, fading from +1.5%+ premarket). Do not chase; wait for pullback to 20,350 (100-hour MA) or close below 20,400 to reassess entry. If support holds and breadth improves (tech outperforms services weakness), re-enter on retest.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings & guidance
factors.summary: Microsoft cutting 4,800 jobs and Xbox downsizing; Meta capex to surge in 2027; Nvidia next-gen AI rack delayed to 2028 per SemiAnalysis. Mixed signals on capital allocation and cost discipline.

factors.name: AI capex & hyperscaler momentum
factors.summary: TeraWulf signed $19B 20-year lease with Anthropic, driving Bitcoin mining and AI infrastructure stocks higher. SK Hynix kicking off $28B US listing to capitalize on surging memory-chip demand. Strong AI infrastructure narrative intact.

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations
factors.summary: Fed's Waller (Rome, 2026-07-06 15:00) reaffirmed 2% inflation commitment and noted risks have 'flipped around' with labor stabilizing. No surprise hawkish/dovish surprise; forward guidance debate ongoing. Soft jobs data last week trimmed rate-hike bets.

factors.name: Real yields & duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Gold consolidating below $4,200 with USD strength offsetting geopolitical repricing. No major TIPS move signaled in news; real yields likely stable. Long-duration tech not under acute repricing pressure.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD gaining vs EUR, GBP, JPY; DXY trimming gains as Fed rate-hike bets fade. Mixed macro backdrop supports modest dollar bid, which is headwind for international-heavy Nasdaq components.

factors.name: Crypto correlation & risk-on sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin volatile: Trump called himself 'big fan of crypto' and Trump Accounts launched today; Strategy sold 3,600 BTC for dividends (bearish signal); Bitmine announced $74M Ether buys. Crypto sentiment bifurcated; Trump pro-crypto comments offset selling pressure.

factors.name: Sector divergence
factors.summary: Tech and healthcare stocks showing 'stark contrast'; healthcare leading. Tech not showing broad-based strength despite AI narrative. Internals weakening relative to index.

factors.name: Technical momentum
factors.summary: Nasdaq tested 200-hour MA repeatedly at end of June/early July without sustained breakout; buyers 'had their shot' but lost momentum per Investinglive (2026-07-06 16:58). Index unable to establish conviction above key level.

factors.name: VIX / volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX spike or crush in news; general risk-off tone from extended weekend break but not panic. SpaceX joining NDX could add gamma/option interest but no positioning squeeze cited.

factors.name: China-Taiwan / geopolitical tail risk
factors.summary: No new China-Taiwan tension or chip export control news in 24h window. Iran ceasefire mentioned but not driving equity repricing. Low headline risk currently.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is consolidating after repeated failed attempts to break above its 200-hour moving average through end of June/early July. While AI infrastructure capex remains robust (TeraWulf/Anthropic, SK Hynix IPO), mega-cap tech earnings are mixed—MSFT job cuts, NVDA rack delays, and sector divergence (healthcare outperforming) suggest internals are weakening despite headline AI strength. Trump's pro-crypto stance and Trump Accounts launch provided a modest risk-on impulse, but Bitcoin volatility and Strategy's BTC sell-off underscore choppy sentiment. Real yields and Fed policy appear stable but not supportive; the index lacks clear directional conviction.
catalysts_ahead: CPI/PCE inflation data (mid-week typical, not specified in news—watch for surprise inflation re-acceleration)
FOMC meeting (TBD, but forward guidance debate suggests market uncertainty on next move)
Samsung earnings (mentioned as pivotal)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL quarterly results (not imminent but critical for bias validation)
SpaceX inclusion effects (fresh optionality, ~500k options traded by midday Monday)
SK Hynix US listing roadshow (memory chip demand signal)
screening.risk_warning: A break above 30000 on strong AI/cloud capex commentary or hawkish Fed surprise would invalidate neutral bias toward BUY6+. Conversely, earnings misses, capex guidance cuts, or inflation surprise would trigger capitulation below 29500 support and shift to SELL. Watch for retest of 200-hour MA as potential inflection; chop in 29500–30000 zone likely for 48–72h.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is consolidating after repeated test of 200-hour MA; avoid chasing either direction into this phase. Wait for a clean break of 29500 (support violation = entry for shorts) or 30000 (resistance break = entry for longs) with volume confirmation. Otherwise, range-bind premium-selling or mean-reversion trades in 29600–29900 zone.

factors.name: US equity market breadth
factors.summary: Nasdaq closed +1.12% (+288.49 pts to 26,121.16) on Monday, outperforming Dow (+0.29%) and S&P 500 (+0.72%). Tech-led rally reflects strength in chipmakers and AI infrastructure momentum.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI capex tailwinds
factors.summary: Broadcom +4% on extended Apple silicon partnership through 2031; TeraWulf shares gained on $19B Anthropic AI infrastructure deal over 20 years. Strong demand signals for AI buildout and chip supply chain.

factors.name: Fed policy shift (dovish surprise emerging)
factors.summary: Fed Governor Waller reaffirmed 2% inflation commitment but traders have repriced softer Fed outlook post-Friday's weak NFP (softer jobs data). DXY gaining despite this, showing technical rather than fundamental USD strength.

factors.name: Real yields & long-duration growth sensitivity
factors.summary: 10-year yields steady with slight risk-off tone visible (gold flat, DXY +0.25%). No major compression in real yields yet; duration-sensitive mega-caps remain in holding pattern pending clearer Fed guidance.

factors.name: Trump administration pro-growth signals
factors.summary: Trump Accounts launched July 4 with crypto-friendly positioning; Trump signaled openness to Bitcoin inclusion; SpaceX CEO stock donation discussions and Toyota $3.6B Texas capex announcement all support risk-on backdrop.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings cycle timing
factors.summary: No major earnings dates flagged in next 24-48h. NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings are weeks away; lack of imminent earnings data removes near-term catalyst clarity.

factors.name: China-AI friction (Alibaba bans Anthropic)
factors.summary: Alibaba placed Anthropic's Claude Code on high-risk software list citing 'distillation attack' concerns. Geopolitical friction around AI could slow cross-border AI capex and cloud adoption in Asia.

factors.name: Microsoft headwind (4,800 job cuts announced)
factors.summary: Microsoft cutting jobs in commercial business and Xbox gaming division where revenue has been shrinking. Weight on mega-cap sentiment despite AI focus elsewhere in portfolio.

factors.name: VIX/volatility regime
factors.summary: No mention of spiking VIX; overnight moves modest (4h: -0.04%, 24h: -0.24%). Index grinding sideways into week, suggesting neither panic nor euphoria currently priced.

factors.name: Dollar strength (risk-off signal)
factors.summary: DXY near 100.90 with traders most positive on USD since 2015 per Bloomberg; strong dollar typically headwind for international-heavy Nasdaq (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings exposure). AUD and GBP under pressure.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq rallied +1.12% on Monday led by chipmakers (Broadcom, AI infrastructure demand signals) and continued enthusiasm around AI capex multi-year cycle. However, price action remains choppy near the 200-hour MA per technicals, with prior BUY6/10 reads failing to trigger confirmation. Fed dovish repricing (softer jobs data) supports equities, but strong USD and Microsoft job cuts temper euphoria. Trump's pro-crypto/pro-growth messaging and extended Apple-Broadcom partnership provide tactical tailwinds, but the index has not decisively broken above resistance.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speaker calendar (Waller continuing this week from Rome)
New Zealand RBNZ decision (July 8) — potential hawkish surprise ripple to USD strength
CPI print (likely mid-week if July calendar consistent with prior years)
FOMC minutes or additional Fed communication could shift rate expectations
NVDA earnings guidance/commentary on AI demand (timing TBD but material catalyst)
Samsung earnings and SK Hynix ADR listing momentum (semiconductor supply chain shifts)
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation if DXY surges above 101.50 on fresh hawkish Fed signals OR if CPI surprise hot (>3.2%), forcing real-yield re-pricing higher and compressing growth multiples. Also watch for China-US AI regulation escalation or Taiwan tension spike, which could derail chipmaker rally and reverse Broadcom/NVDA enthusiasm. Microsoft's job cut announcement could signal broader softening in cloud/AI capex demand if confirmed by peers.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (Monday +1.12% close); Nasdaq testing 200-hour MA per technicals. Wait for retest of 29,400–29,500 support zone before adding longs; if 30,000 resistance broken on volume, reassess for higher targets, but entry now risks paying for a late-phase move into event risk (Fed communications mid-week).
